title: In memoriam: 7 of our favorite Sam Neill films
gist: An obituary and film retrospective for New Zealand actor Sam Neill, who died at 78 in Sydney; the piece is a cultural tribute (Jurassic Park, The Piano, Peaky Blinders, and five other favorite films) with no business or investment content.

themes.theme: Sam Neill obituary and career retrospective

insights.claim: Sam Neill, best known for playing Dr. Alan Grant in Jurassic Park (1993) and Jurassic World Dominion (2022), died on Monday in Sydney, Australia at age 78; he had been treated for stage 3 blood cancer including CAR T-cell therapy this April and was reportedly cancer-free at the time of his 'sudden and unexpected' death.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: No investment relevance; a cultural/celebrity news item with no market, technology, or policy signal for investors.

insights.claim: Neill's four-decade career spanned major franchise work (Jurassic Park), prestige independent film (the Oscar-winning The Piano), and television (Peaky Blinders, Merlin, Reilly Ace of Spies), alongside owning New Zealand's Two Paddocks winery.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: No investment relevance beyond noting his winery business (Two Paddocks), which he described as never having been economically self-sustaining.
insights.quote: I'd like the vineyard to support me, but I'm afraid it's the other way around

insights.claim: Industry figures including Steven Spielberg, Cillian Murphy, Laura Dern, and Taika Waititi publicly paid tribute to Neill following his death, underscoring his cross-generational standing in Hollywood.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: No investment relevance; purely a cultural tribute roundup.
